entry.title: Re: How to show the PO document type key in the "Create Purchase Order-Advanced" App?
entry.content: I found it. It is solved.

entry.title: Re: Treatment of Exchange Rate during Goods receipt

entry.title: Re: Restrict downloading of BO reports from personal devices
entry.content:
Hi Bernardo,

Thanks for reply, we want to restrict download/upload access only when user logged in to public BO url using personal device but need to keep necessary access to same user to download/upload when logged in through company provided device.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Building a docker image of a cds-dk app on a Raspberry Pi crash
entry.content:
Hello, I'm trying to containerise my CAP app to deploy it on a raspberry.

I am able to build the Docker image on windows without any problem.

But, when I try to build on the Raspberry (running Raspberry Os Lite), it gives this crash:

 

133.8 npm error code 132
133.8 npm error path /usr/src/app/node_modules/@sap/cdsdk/node_modules/@sap/hana-client
133.8 npm error command sh -c node checkbuild.js
133.8 npm error command failed
133.8 npm error Illegal instruction (core dumped)
133.8 npm notice
133.8 npm notice New patch version of npm available! 10.8.2 -> 10.8.3
133.8 npm notice Changelog: https://github.com/npm/cli/releases/tag/v10.8.3
133.8 npm notice To update run: pm install -g npm@10.8.3
133.8 npm notice
133.8 npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /root/.npm/_logs/2024-09-25T11_34_13_5742-debug-0.log

 

 Any help is appreciated !

entry.title: Re: Copy and Paste from ClipBoard / Add Item to New Sales...
entry.content:
Hi shai_berkenblit,

Thanks for this, the main issue is we can't use the service layer as we need the process on the creation of the Sales Order. What we are trying to achieve is so that when the user is creating an order for a client they can view the items of the past orders of the client including prices and copy that information to the new sales order lines.

entry.title: Re: How to create a curriculum with item A and following...
entry.content: This is a great option!

entry.title: Re: Report Stories Showing Incorrect Date – Manual Change...
entry.content: The issue was due to the 'Keep last saved values for dynamic variables' box being automatically checked in the Optimised Design View Report.

entry.title: Request for Overhead Absorption Process
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Please provide us with the standard process for overhead absorption during product manufacturing.

I would appreciate your prompt response to this matter.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request for Overhead Absorption Process
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

In the SAP Help Documents below you can check more details about Overhead Absorption Process: 

Overhead Absorption by Production Lots
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cec3b9f722d1014a669db05eefa0809.html?locale=en-US

Overhead Absorption by Cost Objects
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2c217b87722d101485f586ea8749486b.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards,  
Ihan
SAP Product Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign:
https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content: It sets only the current user. I would like to apply my settings to all users

entry.title: Re: Apple In-App Purchase - How do I...
entry.content:
Hello Verent,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Unfortunately, we have never supported custom SDKs or in-app purchases, and I don't believe it’s possible at this time. I haven’t encountered the workarounds you mentioned, but given Apple's strict policies on how builds are made, I doubt they would be compatible.

However, if you have details on the workarounds, I’d be interested in reviewing them.

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle
entry.content: You really dialed down the difficulty this time. No hidden clues, no Obama, no holiday APIs.  🙂

entry.title: User Plan system configuration setting learningPlanRequiredItemsDisplayThreshold behavior
entry.content:
It was presented that the User Plan system configuration setting "learningPlanRequiredItemsDisplayThreshold" when enabled in the new learning experience will hide all free-floating required items without a due date.

Can we then force a due date on required assignements in the system to avoid that ?

Or will those items be displayed in another section of the learning page ? ("Invest in Myself" for exemple).

We don't understand the logic, if we need the property to hide assignements made by the admins or the system and due in more than 6 months, but the managers assign a required training without a due date, does that mean that the employee won't see it ?

Also, if we only want to hide learnings attached to Goals (Career Development Plan) that have the plan's due date, does that mean that all the other required assignements made in the LMS without a due date will never be displayed to the employee ?

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Time-Based Filtering in SAC Stories: Using Model Variables for Measure Restriction
entry.content: Hi @Oleks_Master , yes, that will work just fine. The restriction is on the property.

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle
entry.content: Yes, I wanted more people to play, and also there are a lot of people whose native language is not English and cryptic crosswords I figured was unfair to them.

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @DWE ,

Thank you for your feedback on the initial release of the SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint. We understand your concerns about the current limitations

This add-in is based on the new widget API in the lightweight viewer (LWV), which means it supports features available in LWV. We are actively working on expanding this support and have the following features planned in our roadmap:
- Support for stories created with scripting
- Integration with stories that have mandatory prompts and variables
- Support for table and geo widgets

We appreciate your patience and valuable feedback as it helps us prioritize our development efforts

Regards,
Ashok R

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate the ISBD status in projects?
entry.content: Hi, thanks for reply the message, yes I have created a case for SAP:)

entry.title: How to load the data to non prod HDI container tables created using CAPM from Qa/dev environment.
entry.content: We have a new Landscape coming up and need to move data from SAP HANA cloud HDI containers designed using CAPM app to another environment as when the CAPM app is deployed the HDI configuration is done and Tables are generated . But now need to load the data into table. How to upload data is there any process?

entry.title: Re: Is there any SAP Note/KBA for Pre-requisites to check...
entry.content:
SAP has two products that could be considered as successor of the IPC: SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (aka CPS) and SAP Solution Sales Configuration (SSC). It depends on the use case which of the products is most suitable for you. Simplified one could say: single-level configuration and multi-level configuration with BOM -> CPS; multi-level configuration without BOM (bottom-up system configuration) -> SSC.

This note might help: 2711932 - System Requirements for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing

entry.title: Re: Hana Migration Cockpit Direct Transfer Error
entry.content: Hello, Have you come with a solution to the problem? We face the same error in S4HANA Cloud 2023 Private edition. Despite the profit center being defined in the template, the error seems to persist from the majority of the materials.

entry.title: Customer Course GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services) Available for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
The GW100 got a “technical update”, which means that all exercises are now running on the newest SAP S/4HANA available today. In addition, the slides now follow the newest design guidelines of SAP. So, welcome to this short blog post about the customer training GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services).

If you are more interested in a digital learning for SAP Gateway, please read my blog post:
SAP Learning Journey – Building OData Services with SAP Gateway

Introduction

Starting in 2014, this is now the eighth incarnation of how to develop OData services with SAP Gateway. The course shows all parts of SAP Gateway, which are available since SAP NetWeaver Application Server 7.0 (SAP Gateway 2.0), as well as parts added up to SAP S/4HANA 2023. The whole bandwidth of SAP Gateway covering all releases is shown.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. SAP Gateway Overview
  2. OData Overview
  3. SAP Gateway Service Implementation
  4. SAP Gateway Service Generation
  5. SAP Gateway Service Redefinition
  6. SAP Gateway and CDS Views
  7. SAP Gateway Hub Functionalities
  8. Advanced OData Options
  9. SAP Gateway Security
  10. Further Information

If customers are interested, they can order a customer specific version of the course including only those topics suitable for their release or purpose.

System Landscape

Let’s start with the system release: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Our system landscape – we call it universal target – is used in many technology courses and offers full access for the participants from the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP) in the browser down to SAP HANA on the SUSE Linux Enterprise server (SLES). Everything is set up following the newest guidelines of SAP so that it can really act as a template for customers.

GW100 System Landscape (Screenshot from System Setup Guide)

In addition, we offer an instance of the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) to show the consumption of OData services. If you want to know more about this topic, check out our courses for SAP Fiori development.

Example: Business Services

As an example from the material, here a shorten excerpt about defining business services.

Business Services: OData V2 and V4 (Screenshot from GW100)

In the area of business services, a service definition exposes CDS Views as a generic service. A service binding then marks it as a special kind of service. You can create an OData V2 or V4 service for API- or UI-usage. Service definitions and bindings are created in the ADT, the generated OData services are registered in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or /IWFND/V4_ADMIN respectively.

How to Create a Service Definition and Binding (Screenshot from GW100)

Service definitions and bindings are ABAP repository objects and a substantial part of the business services. They can only be created and edited in the ADT. In the first step, the service definition just defines the name of the service. The CDS views are added later in the source code.

In the second step, the service binding refers to the service definition and sets the binding type, which are mainly communication protocols.

Service Definition and Binding Correlation (Screenshot from GW100)

By exposing a CDS View in a service definition, the name of the entity set can be defined. For the entity type, "Type" is automatically appended to the entity set name.

The entity set names and navigation properties based on the associations in the CDS views are visible in the service binding on the right. For OData V2, multiple versions of a service definition can be added to a service binding visible on the left. For OData V4, multiple service definitions can be added to one service binding to allow cross-service navigation.


Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/GW100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Some parts of the GW100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/building-odata-services-with-sap-gateway

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the GW100 in any kind, you found some useful information around SAP Gateway in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Integration of Data Action and Multi Action into File Repository (Part II)
entry.content: Hi @ChenNee already checked that but no folders are listed at all. So, I don't understand the warning message when deleting the generated teams.

entry.title: Re: OBYC BSX GL account P&L
entry.content:
I do not think so we can create good receipt posting with P&L GL assigned into OBYC anymore into S/4 HANA. when you try to post GR then system will throw an error stating that " Account 555100 (P&L GL) is not a balance sheet account so as per this, we will have to maintain spare parts as a inventory into system and issue spare parts at time of consumption (i.e. Good issue to Maintenance order).

 

Thank you,

Regards,

Abhishek Jaiswal

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Reconciliation (Customer/Vendor: Process 003)
entry.content: Thank you 🙂

entry.title: 🔵 Serving Fine-Tuned Models in Production
entry.content:
When it comes to serving of fine-tuned LLMs it is essential in production systems to share the base models, while hosting the LoRA parameters in the same container. This reduces the costs, however poses new problems like providing tenant isolation, scaling and dynamic loading of new adapters. In this talk we will cover the different aspects of the SAP Generative AI Hub and how the serving issues can be solved.

Speaker: Karim Mohraz, SAP

I need to add a button on Customer Main Page, I have already created the customization and extended the View, but I need to know how to enhance the class 

cl_bs_bp_guibb_tax_numbers 

to use my custom process_event method to handle the button behavior.

Thanks,

Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Eclipse, Field for creation user is missing in source...
entry.content:
Edit: Just editing my comment to say that this solution worked for me too. First time around, I didn't realise that I needed to change the data type on the right rather than the field name on the left, which is why it didn't work, but as soon as I switched obviously all was well.

Many thanks for all your efforts.

Nikhil

entry.title: How to unlock Team Potential with Organization and Relationship Systems Coaching?
entry.content:
In ORSC we distinguish three interconnected forms of intelligence that are important when we engage in groups:

  • Emotional Intelligence describes the relationship with ourselves and our capacity to articulate and respond to our personal emotions.
    – How do I dance?

  • Social Intelligence describes our relationship with others and how we can empathize with them.
    – What is it like to dance together?

  • Relationship Systems Intelligence describes the relationships across the team, group, or system and what emerges from the collective.
    – What is it in the dance that emerges among us?

Relationship Systems Intelligence

Principles of Relationship Systems Intelligence

The following principles of Relationship Systems Intelligence are the foundation of the ORSC framework which does not focus on individuals but on the system like e.g. a team that they are part of:

  • Each relationship system has its own unique identity.
    – If you were to describe the team as a person, what characteristics would it have?

  • Each member of the relationship system is a voice of the system.
    – What perspectives within the team haven't been fully heard?

  • Relationship systems are inherently intelligent, generative and creative.
    – What patterns and shifts do you experience in the team?

  • Relationship systems rely on roles to organize and perform functions.
    – How are the different roles serving the team as a whole?

  • Relationship systems are in a constant state of emergence.
    – What are ways we can embrace changes to foster a continuous development of the team?

 

Meta-Skills for Coaching Relationship Systems

Coaching always has a being and a doing aspect. The best intervention is not effective if the atmosphere is not right. Thus, ORSC coaches also focus on the “come from” place from which the work is done and create intentional emotional fields using the following meta-skills:

  • The Heart aspect focuses on building strong, authentic relationships within a team to create a foundation of trust and empathy.
    – What actions can you take to deepen your relationships and build more trust and empathy within your team?

  • Deep Democracy emphasizes the importance of hearing all voices and perspectives within the system, ensuring inclusive decision-making and valuing diverse viewpoints.
    – Which perspective might be missing, and how can we make sure everyone feels heard?

  • Playfulness encourages a light-hearted and curious approach to challenges encouraging open exploration.
    – How would a magician solve the problem?

  • Respect is honouring each team member’s unique contributions, fostering an environment of mutual appreciation.
    – What do you appreciate about each team members contributions?

  • Collaboration and Partnership are valuing each team member's strengths and promoting cooperative efforts toward shared goals.
    – How can we leverage the strengths of each team member?

  • Enquiry and Awareness encourage curiosity to peel the onion of what is happening.
    – What is happening now? And now?

  • Commitment is required to take over responsibility, and follow-through in achieving a team’s objectives.
    – What specific commitments are we willing to make to ensure we achieve our shared objectives?

  • The Other aspect fosters an understanding that a team is part of a larger systems that impact its functioning.
    – What external factors might be influencing your team, and how should you consider these in your strategy?

 

Dimensions of Relationship Systems

The three dimensions in the middle of the ORSC model indicate how a relationship system expresses itself:

  • The Intelligence dimension recognizes that every system has an innate ability to self-organize, solve problems, and generate innovative solutions. An ORSC coach can support a team to access its own collective intelligence to live its full potential.
    – What is trying to happen?

  • The Geography dimension explores spaces within a relationship system that shapes its interactions. An ORSC coach can help a team map out these different territories to better understand how they affect communication and collaboration.
    – Which perspectives within the team are left out?

  • The Path dimension focuses on the journey of the relationship system over time. An ORSC coach can enable a team to explore past successes and challenges, to clarify current goals and values, and to envision a shared future.
    – What would success look like for your team and how will we know you have arrived at this future?

 

Workshop Insights

As an ORSC coach, my role is not to fix or repair a system, but rather to illuminate the inherent dynamics, strengths, and patterns already present within it. By facilitating a space where the system's natural tendencies and underlying structures are revealed, a shared experience is created that brings awareness to hidden potential and unseen opportunities for growth.

As a client of a team workshop I recently held sums it up: "Thanks to you, we shared all together a precious moment, full of emotion, energy and fun. You offered a safe space. You gently guided us to connect with ourselves and express our creativity. You helped us listen to all voices, realize our strengths, reflect on our needs and build on our commonalities and our experience. In our daily lives, we tend to forget them. It's good to remember that we have a solid foundation on which to build."

 

Originally published on my blog imagium.de

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG Changing worklist content failed mass processing
entry.content: hi @ashishmishra_official  i checked but there is no error.

entry.title: Re: Not getting data and 401 Unauthorize error in Mobile...
entry.content: A 401 error indicates the backend does not accept the credentials. The next step would be to check the backend logs to see exactly what user info has been passed that is being rejected and check for error messages in the backend to see if it tells you what is not correct

entry.title: Re: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigation in SAP MDK

entry.title: Transforming Your HR Operating Model with SAP SuccessFactors Implementation
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, staying ahead requires not just innovation but a keen understanding of operational processes, technology, and adaptable operating models.

On Thursday 12th September 2024, Dr Andreas Eckert, Global HR Advisor, and Klaus Kampen, SAP’s Business Architect for HR Innovation and Transformation, delved into the potential impact and opportunities for your HR operating model through the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors. 

For us at SAP, success is when customers actually realize the value that they expect from their investment in our solutions. But obviously value doesn't realize itself. A solution itself doesn't provide you with the value. It's important to design the processes that actually drive the system configuration, with some principles in mind on how they best perform the best in your specific context. 

Important processes though, are not only just things that happen along a chain of a chain of events. Processes also include the actors that are actively contributing and doing tasks along these processes. So the operating model of HR informs and must inform the process design. And this is where the concept of SAP’s leading practices comes in.

SAP SuccessFactors has a number of leading practices across all solution areas that actually encapsulate and condensate consulting experience and combine that with product development and design principles to inform process design to inform the operating model duties and help move forward. 

The webinar focussed on the 1st building block out of the critical steps chain that need to be taken to arrive to the point of value: 

Key Insights from the Webinar:

  • Learning from Top Performers: Using a sports example, we explored how leading organizations use SAP SuccessFactors to streamline HR processes and drive business success.
  • Impact of Leading Practices: SAP SuccessFactors’ best practices provide a strong foundation for transforming HR functions across consultancy, expertise, and operations. (Independently from your particular HR Operating Model) There are 5 essential functions that exist in all HR Operating Modell that assume responsibilities and that will be impacted by the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors
  • Real-Life Customer Example: We demonstrated how a customer successfully applied SAP SuccessFactors leading practice recommendations to overcome manual processes, scale HR operations, and align with their growth strategy.
  • Workshop Insights: Through our Impact and Opportunity workshops, we highlighted the importance of mapping roles and responsibilities to ensure clarity and efficiency in process design. 
  • Process Design and Configuration: We emphasized how defined tasks and roles translate into detailed process diagrams, which guide the technical configuration of SAP SuccessFactors.
  • HR Leading Practice Advisory Service: We introduced our advisory service, which supports organizations in aligning their HR models with SAP SuccessFactors best practices for a smooth and tailored implementation.

The implementation of SAP SuccessFactors can elevate your HR operations by aligning HR with business goals, automating key processes, enhancing employee experience, and enabling data-driven decision-making. Organizations that are prepared to embrace this change will unlock new opportunities for growth, innovation, and efficiency.

HR Leading Practices Advisory Service is part of the SAP SuccessFactors Services portfolio that aims to support customers interested in applying leading practices to elevate their own Operating Model and achieve value expected from the SAP SuccessFactors features and function by: 

  • Defining the right HR concepts and select the right scope 
  • Evaluating the potential change impact on your HR organization
  • Prepare a smooth SAP SuccessFactors implementation with a well- equipped project team that has made key design decisions in advance 

If you are interested in learning more, please don’t hesitate to reach out to: SAPHIT@SAP.com

Resources: 

Webinar Recording

Slides

entry.title: Re: NSQ Meetup on Thu, Sept 26 - Who is in / Where to meet?
entry.content:
We're holding our meetup at UNO Pizzeria & Grill, across the street from SAP's NSQ offices, on Thursday, September 26, at 6:00 p.m. Get more details -- and register -- here: https://community.sap.com/t5/newtown-square-events/sap-community-meetup-newtown-square-thu-sept-26/ev-p/13876539

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Re: How to use I_JournalEntryTP in custom RAP BO
entry.content:
" I_JournalEntryTP~Post is defined as Save Action in RAP which can only be called during a specified RAP saver mehtod. Otherwise leads to a short dump"

@TakeZhou 

It's not possbile to create  a custom action to Post Document instead of Save Action?

entry.title: Re: Walkthrough SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring
entry.content: Cannot see "process authoring with Signavio" in Cloud ALM. How do I get it?

entry.title: Migration of Asset values in mid-year migration with migration cockpit
entry.content:
In the context of data migration via staging tables approach, how to maintain APC values for assets created/activated in the current year?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration of Asset values in mid-year migration with...
entry.content:
Each data migration object has an extensive documentation available for both S/4HANA Cloud Public AND Private editions.

Regarding APC posting, the documentation for "Fixed asset - Postings" states:

  • With transfers at the end of the fiscal year, the following applies:

    • Cumulative Values worksheet:

      For a transfer at the end of the fiscal year, the cumulated acquisition and production costs (APC) and the cumulated depreciation values are transferred. These are the values up to the transfer date at the end of the year. You enter this data on the Cumulative Values worksheet.

  • With transfers during the year, the following applies:

    • Cumulative Values worksheet:

      The cumulated APC and the cumulated depreciation values are transferred. These are the values up to year-end before the transfer date.

entry.title: Profit center in settlement document in CCM
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a query regarding profit center in settlement documents.

When we create a Customer Invoice in intercompany scenario, profit center does not get populated in the invoice (VBRP-PRCTR). It is blank. The line items will not have the profit center from the material.

Since profit center is blank in customer invoice, settlement documents will have dummy profit center or blank profit center. Expectation is to have profit center same as the one in material.

The scenario works fine for standard O2C flow.

Any help/inputs regarding this would be very helpful.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Elevate Your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Architect for SAP BTP
entry.content: @kapil_kr_gupta Thanks for asking! The exam consists of multiple choice, multiple response, drag and drop questions. To prepare for the exam, we recommend taking the above mentioned learning journey and then you should be all set!

entry.title: Re: Walkthrough SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring
entry.content:
Hello CHINNABATHINA,

There is no Process Authoring with Signavio available in SAP Cloud ALM. can you please tell in which documentation you found this?

Thanks!

entry.title: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in MSA database level replication.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can anyone help me in understanding the exact internal behaviour of 'dump marker' and 'resync marker' with respect to MSA database level replication? 

Questions:

1. How the Replication Server will come to know about the transactions that happened before writing the dump marker to the PDB's log to be replicated or not if the scenario is to resynchronize the RDB without dropping the existing RDB connection?

2. In MSA database replication in order to resynchronize the RDB, 'resync marker' is the perfect option to be followed instead of dump marker?

Regards,
Dilip Voora

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA 2020 embedded EWM Automatic Goods Receipt Posting
entry.content: Use Group DLVIMFG

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Integration: Zipping Multiple Files in SAP CPI
entry.content: Thanks

entry.title: Re: What data can you use Bulk Uploads for?
entry.content: As I understand, Bulk Uploads is used to mass upload attachments to the employee profiles. 'Import and Export Data' can be used to make mass changes to employee records using given templates.

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Integration: Zipping Multiple Files in SAP CPI
entry.content: how to Set sap cpi zip file password

entry.title: Re: Time Calculation Data Action - From To Period
entry.content:
Thanks that was exactly what I was looking for!

However, even though the property is "text" I realized that the format is wrong as it is in mm.dd.yyyy and SAC only accepts yyyy.dd.mm format...

Do you have a workaround for this?

entry.title: How to work with test cases and test plans in several rollouts?
entry.content:
Hello,

I´m working with a customer, where we will implement SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud on several sites in different rollouts. We have created some standard test cases for the first rollout that we want to reuse for upcoming rollouts.

We've created a new scope for each rollout/site. Is it possible to copy the content of an already existing test plan to use in the next rollout?

What's your suggestion on how to work with test plans? Should we create a test plan for each module, for each module and rollout, or do you suggest something else?

Thanks in advance!

BR,

Ebba

entry.title: MASS transaction for Dispute Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

 

I have a requirement that

1. Create Dispute cases and assign dispute reasons 

2. Update Dispute reasons for currently opened Dispute Cases

User is looking for MASS transaction or LSMW to create or update the above requests. Is there any way to solve the issue.

entry.title: Re: What data can you use Bulk Uploads for?
entry.content: Do you have any examples of what can be updated? Or know any KBA's we can use to help with this?

entry.title: SAP Business Application Studio doesn't connect to our On-Premise System / AxiosError 403
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Unfortunately I cannot select the services from our SAP system and get the error:

Authentication incorrect AxiosError: Request failed with status code 403

Everything is already configured in the Cloud Connector and in the BTP and most of it seems to be correct

The communicationchek in the BTP is successful

The SAP Cloud Connector also works and seems to be set up correctly for me:

Once the settings for the Cloud To On-Premise configuration

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano  

I am Bui Bao Phuoc, my current job is as a solution implementation consultant for a technology company in Vietnam. Throughout my studies and work, I have gained practical experience in consulting on ERP solutions to help businesses manage resources and operate their companies effectively in the current digital age

Recently, I have become increasingly interested in expanding my digital skills, especially in ERP systems, and I am looking forward to further enhancing my understanding of SAP. I believe that my strong analytical thinking, business mindset and commitment to continuous improvement make me a good fit for the SAP curriculum.

I am excited about the opportunity to join this digital skills initiative and look forward to contributing my enthusiasm and skills to the learning journey.

Best regards,

Phuoc Bui

entry.title: Business Objects 4.3 Upgrade from 4.2.9 -- License workflow
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm going to be upgrading our 4.2.9 clustered environment to 4.3 (in-place).  I am aware of the need for new licenses.  What I'm unsure of is when I need to apply the licenses and the impact of not having licenses even if temporary.  I've read that I should remove the old keys first and then add new keys:    

  • Do I remove the old licenses before I even start the 4.3 upgrade? 
    • If so, what if I can't do the upgrade when planned?  Can I still get into the environment with no licenses associated with it?  Would the SIA start?

entry.title: Pre approval for overtime in Time Management
entry.content:
Dear community, 

Our customer would like to build a pre-approval process for the Managers to request their collaborators to do some overtime. 

We have built something similar with extra time types, but they would like to give the manager the option to ask the employee to perform an amount of hours of overtime in advance, and then the employee would decide when and how distribute them. Instead of the manager deciding when, the employee would adapt his work hours to perform that overtime along the week. 

Example: 

Manager: I want you to work next week +5 hours. 

Employee: would work his theoretical working time and would decide to perform the overtime:

  • tuesday from 17 to 18 +1 hour
  • wednesday from 17 to 20 +3 hours
  • friday from 16 to 17 +1 hour. 

Is it somehow feasible to build in Time Management?

Thank you in advance,

Laura

entry.title: Re: What data can you use Bulk Uploads for?
entry.content:
If you go to Manage Data and enter either 'Import and Export' or 'Import Employee Data', you can review the templates available.

There is also 'Manage Mass Changes' to make mass changes to employee data.

entry.title: Re: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigat...
entry.content: Hi Bill, I can provide more details and screenshots to explain my use case, which will help you understand it better. I have a page called 'Delivery List,' which is my first page and triggers all the related functions during the page load. On this page, I have a count and a log icon, both of which should update dynamically whenever there is a change in the item count or any error logs. This works fine on the Delivery page, but it’s not updating on the Customer page. I tried using data subscriptions and added the entity, but the Customer page(Inactive Page) is still not refreshing when the entity changes. As an alternative, I found that navigating to a new page and refreshing it upon opening causes all mapped rules to be recalled and updates the UI accordingly (e.g., the count and the log icon color changing to red when a log is present). This approach works fine for all pages where I use a redraw on successful navigation, except for the 'Customer List' and 'Delivery List.' Here, navigation happens via the side drawer, and the previous page is refreshed. For example, when navigating from the Customer page to the Delivery page, the Customer page gets refreshed. Ideally, the Delivery page should be refreshed to update the rules and UI. Because of this, even though the count is 0, the UI is not updating. I’ve attached a screenshot which will help explain this better.

entry.title: Re: How to do a lookup of a field in another model in SAC
entry.content: Sorry for asking the basic question. Thanks for the feedback.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM vs SAP Solution Manager
entry.content:
Nice day.
It is possible to have an instance of Solman 7.2 in the cloud and collaboratively work with C ALM.
The objective of this application is: With the SAP SOLMAN72 ALM engines, such as: ALM, FOCUSED BUILD – FOCUSE RUN - FOCUSED INSIGTH, integrate and collaborate all the SYSTEMS and the LANDSCAPE that will be on-Premise and non-SAP and in a collaborative scenario [SAP SOLMAN 72 ALM < –- > SAP CLOUD ALM] with the entire PLATFORM AND SOLUTIONS in the CLOUD, are integrated with the ARCHITECTURAL tools and frameworks of the ALM Methodology. If this is possible, does it carry a cost ?

entry.title: Re: Foreign exchange gain/loss on GR on purchase order
entry.content:
There are many Customizing to analyze in your system such as exchange rate type, Did you already consider the Invoice Posting with a third exchange rate?

Did you already read 308008 - FAQ: Posting logic: GR/IR clearing account or 2684884 - Posting logic for exchange rate differences (moving average price material) ?

entry.title: Crystal Report Viewer Chart Font Issue
entry.content:
I created a report in crystal report 2020 and it contains charts.

the font for the charts axis's are arial and when i preview it in the designer it looks good.

When the report gets loaded in visual studio report viewer the fonts on the charts are all congested and bolded.

I'm using SP36.

 

GoodBad

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
@HarveyXu,

A global or rule on legal entity is not enough. At least for The Netherlands it needs to be possible to make the name format for the display name selectable on employee level. This as the employee can choose between their birth name, partner name and combinations of the two to be used in the display name. This is possible for the formal name, not the display name.

See also Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

entry.title: Readiness Check: Maintenance Planner Error
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Can someone provide guidance on what actions need to be taken when the Add-On Compatibility and Active Business Functions analyses in the SAP Readiness Check indicate that "Maintenance Planner Status is Error"?

Is there any documentation I can refer to for this issue?

Best regards,
Kaiwen

SAP Readiness Check

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint
entry.content: @MortenWittrock ☝️

entry.title: Usage of "JustAsk" within the story in a separate panel.
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

Good day!!

Requirement : User wanted to create charts by just giving prompts in text. We know that we have this feature in JustAsk.

Is it possible to use the JustAsk within the Story page as a separate panel? Instead of clicking the JustAsk Button.

I searched for the APIs for JustAsk but not able to find any.

Thank you for reading through!! 🙂

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM support for Oracle databases
entry.content:
Hi

We are considering moving our Operations from Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM  (CALM).

One of the question for which I do not have a clear answer is the monitoring of an Oracle database. The documentation is clear that on Hana databases are supported.

 

Our systems meet all of the requirements on SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP (7.40 or higher) incl. SAP BW/4HANA. 

 

Do I understand correctly that we can monitor the application servers but not the database servers?  

If that is the case then clearly only systems running on Hana can migrate to CALM?

 

Thanks 


Gavin

entry.title: Re: Custom events trigger from S/4 Hana to SAP BTP Event Mesh
entry.content: This is great - thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigat...
entry.content: The thing to remember is that each drawer is it's own navigation stack. Meaning if you open Customers and then go back to Deliveries when you come back to Customers it isn't really navigating to the page. The page was still "open" in memory and it is switching back to the drawer stack. This is observed if you navigate to a customer detail page for example and then switch to Deliveries and then back you will still be on the Customer Detail page.  You can change this by setting ClearHistory to true which forces the drawer to reload back to the starting point when switching .  It will depend on your use case and desired behaviors.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano . My name is Nikita Agrawal. I completed the SAP learning journey "Configuring SAP ARIBA Procurement" on 12th September 2024. I have sent an email to digitalskills@sap.com with a completed screenshot. Please advise on getting the redemption voucher for the certification associated with this learning.

Thanks 

Nikita Agrawal

entry.title: Custom PDF attachment to JE via API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV - Conversion issue
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

We have a requirement to display and attach custom Adobe Form to Journal Entry. We have built custom Adobe form using Form Service by Adobe in BTP and created RAP application with custom action. We are using S4 HANA Public Cloud system.
 
To generate/render the PDF, we are using cl_fp_ads_util=>render_pdf which provide pdf stream data, and observed that we are getting data in Hexadecimal format.


Now, we want to attach this pdf to Journal Entry, so we are using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV via http call. However, when we pass same pdf stream data to API, we are getting status as 200 (instead of 201 created). Since API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV accept the data in binary format (using set_binary method of http request ) we are facing challenges converting the hex data to binary and successfully attaching the file.

Could anyone guide us on how to resolve this?
Thank you in advance!!

entry.title: Re: Pre approval for overtime in Time Management
entry.content:
Hello Laura,

this is a classical use case for so-called "attendance time accounts". This is not yet in place, but on the roadmap. Concepts are ready, if we are lucky.....and this means: no comitments here....we can build it in H1 release. But don´t nail me on this - depending on the speed of development and priority of other topics this could also spill over into H2 release. So, I think we can say: it is planned for 2025 ;-). 

With the extra time type you won´t be able to cover this - as here the manager needs to state the exact start / end time or day. The extra time type can only be seen as a workaround till the proper attendance accounts are in place - and in your case it does not even help.

Best regards

Volker

entry.title: Re: Access a property in message mapping from a content m...
entry.content:
you don't need a script anymore, there is a standard function provided in the mapping editor to access properties and headers

Alex

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM support for monitoring Content server
entry.content:
Hi

We are considering moving our Operations from Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM  (CALM).

One of the questions for which I do not have a clear answer is the monitoring of Content Server.

 

As I understand it Content Server will be supported for used with S/4Hana. Therefore it will be available in the landscape after Solution Manager reaches the end of mainstream maintenance at the end of 2027.

 

Any advice / guidance / input will be appreciated.

 

Gavin

entry.title: Re: MASS transaction for Dispute Management
entry.content:
Did you already consider

  • Using a BAPI such as BAPI_DISPUTE_CREATE
  • Using the mass options of transaction UDM_DISPUTE (find followed by change field content, relevant customizing here)

entry.title: Re: How to set up enhanced SAP Events for Advanced Event Mesh with Application Interface Framework
entry.content:
Update: The Note with the AddOn Artifacts has been released: https://me.sap.com/notes/3486106 

"

Following artefacts are delivered to support the integration:

Function Module: /AIF/AIF_ACTION_CALL_AEM - AIF Action FM to integrate SAP SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh via HTTP Rest.
Function Module: /AIF/TRANSFER_TO_AIF_RECEIVER - Receiver Function Module for the integration of the AIF runtime in transaction swe2
Function Moudles /AIF/AEM_GET_ID, /AIF/AEM_GET_SOURCE, /AIF/AEM_GET_TIME - AIF Mapping FMs to support the creation of header information of a cloud event
View /AIF/NAME_MAP (maintainable via sm30): view to define the name mapping from ABAP to JSOn."

entry.title: Re: Team owner - Manage Teams and Responsibilities
entry.content: Hello Expert, I am also looking for a solution. How to update USR21-IDADTYPE & USR21-GUID?

entry.title: Re: Take the Training on Cloud Control Center for SAP Business One!
entry.content:
Hi @Keren_Erez 

We're trying to register a new user and it's failing. We got invalid code.

Could you please assist?

 


Thanks,
Paulo

entry.title: API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV how to update ContractNetPriceAmount
entry.content:
Hello,
I would like to change the ContractNetPriceAmount for an existing item. How can I do this?
For example, if I enter

“ContractNetPriceAmount” : “66.00”

in the payload, the value is not changed. I have also debugged this and found out that if a changed value does not match the old value, it is set back to the old value.
This means that the code already prevents me from changing the value in this way.

My question is: how can I change the ContractNetPriceAmount? Is there another way to do it?

I would be grateful for any help as I have been working on this for a long time and have simply not found a way.

entry.title: Re: Team owner - Manage Teams and Responsibilities
entry.content: Hello Medeni, have you found any solution for your last reply? I am facing same issue.

entry.title: Re: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigat...
entry.content: I've tried using clear history true and closing the page on unload, which should reload the page when opened the next time , but it's still not refreshing the new page

entry.title: Cleanup after migrating a system from Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi

 

We are testing SAP Cloud ALM  (CALM) as a replacement of Solution Manager.

 

Thus far the experience has been good with setup well documented and the user experience of Operations being good.

 

One process I have not yet found documented is disconnecting systems from Solution Manager once they are connected to CALM. Therefore, at the moment, the pilot system is sending information to both Solution Manager and CALM.  

Also, should they be removed from the SLD and LMDB once they're connected to CALM? What of SDCC - should there be any changes to this configuration.

Thank you in advance of any pointers / advice /suggestions.

 

Gavin

entry.title: Re: Usage Decision Status Reversal for Non - stock relevant inspection.
entry.content:
I've implemented the SAP note so we are able to reverse the UD.

I have still one issue. When I reverse the UD the system is not allowing to take a new UD to the same inspection lot. Is it the same for you?

entry.title: ETIMEDOUT for SAP CAP App on BTP with external Postgres DB
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have a SAP CAP based application running on BTP CF. We are using an external Postgres DB for storing our data. This Postgres DB runs in a docker container which on a VM hosted on a dedicated HETZNER server.

From time to time (~10-60 mins) we see TCP connection timeouts in the SAP CAP backend like this:

 

2024-09-16T07:41:56.145+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [odata] - POST /odata/v4/api/fawkes/$batch
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [odata] - > currentUser currentUser()
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - Instantiated User Service
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - getOrCreateUser --> Start
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - Checking DB for employee with id 3229
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [2024-09-16T07:43:46.791Z] [31mERROR[39m    Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at TCP.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:217:20)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at TCP.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:128:17)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.795+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR [cds] - ️Uncaught Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at TCP.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:217:20)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at TCP.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:128:17) {
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR errno: -110,
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR code: 'ETIMEDOUT',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR syscall: 'read'
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR }
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR [cds] - Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at /home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/pg/lib/client.js:526:17
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async Object.all (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/postgres/lib/PostgresService.js:174:26)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService._checkCollation (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/postgres/lib/PostgresService.js:97:21)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async Promise.all (index 1)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService.begin (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/db-service/lib/common/DatabaseService.js:70:7)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService._begin [as dispatch] (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-tx.js:199:3) {
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR errno: -110,
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR code: 'ETIMEDOUT',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR syscall: 'read',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR query: "SELECT collname FROM pg_collation WHERE collname = 'en-x-icu';\n ^",
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR id: '1397371',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR level: 'ERROR',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR timestamp: 1726472626799
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR }
2024-09-16T07:43:46.810+0000 [RTR/1] STDOUT XXX-fawkes-backend.cfapps.eu30.hana.ondemand.com - [2024-09-16T07:41:56.136234108Z] "POST /odata/v4/api/fawkes/$batch HTTP/1.1" 200 395 324 "https://XXX-approuter.cfapps.eu30.hana.ondemand.com/XXX/index.html" "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/128.0.0.0 Safari/537.36" "10.1.8.0:30362" "10.1.9.9:61175" x_forwarded_for:"45.158.172.157, 10.1.8.1, 34.159.201.78, 10.1.8.0" x_forwarded_proto:"https" vcap_request_id:"72ddb4ca-6d57-4f41-5353-47909f3e9818" response_time:110.674250 gorouter_time:0.000126 app_id:"29f701b7-10bd-4dfe-9ea0-c1a1b6d3189b" app_index:"0" instance_id:"a2de1dff-2ea3-4cc2-47db-6e54" failed_attempts:0 failed_attempts_time:"-" dns_time:0.000000 dial_time:0.000000 tls_time:0.000000 backend_time:110.674125 x_cf_routererror:"-" x_correlationid:"72ddb4ca-6d57-4f41-5353-47909f3e9818" tenantid:"fawkes" sap_passport:"-" x_scp_request_id:"10e6c249-8c08-469b-8e4b-9bcf732f1271-66E7E144-9DE41A" x_cf_app_instance:"-" x_forwarded_host:"XXX-approuter.cfapps.eu30.hana.ondemand.com" x_custom_host:"-" x_ssl_client:"-" x_ssl_client_session_id:"-" x_ssl_client_verify:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_subject_cn:"-" x_ssl_client_issuer_dn:"-" x_ssl_client_notbefore:"-" x_ssl_client_notafter:"-" x_cf_forwarded_url:"-" traceparent:"-" true_client_ip:"-" x_request_id:"-" x_b3_traceid:"72ddb4ca6d574f41535347909f3e9818" x_b3_spanid:"535347909f3e9818" x_b3_parentspanid:"-" b3:"72ddb4ca6d574f41535347909f3e9818-535347909f3e9818"
2024-09-16T07:43:48.138+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR npm notice
2024-09-16T07:43:48.138+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR npm notice New minor version of npm available! 10.7.0 -> 10.8.3
2024-09-16T07:43:48.138+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR npm notice Changelog: https://github.com/npm/cli/releases/tag/v10.8.3
2024-09-16T07:43:48.138+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR npm notice To update run: npm install -g npm@10.8.3
2024-09-16T07:43:48.138+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR npm notice
2024-09-16T07:43:48.173+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT Exit status 0
2024-09-16T07:43:48.184+0000 [CELL/SSHD/0] STDOUT Exit status 0
2024-09-16T07:43:53.700+0000 [CELL/0] STDOUT Cell 80e41736-2c4c-42a7-9cd7-971d529a7321 stopping instance a2de1dff-2ea3-4cc2-47db-6e54
2024-09-16T07:43:53.700+0000 [CELL/0] STDOUT Cell 80e41736-2c4c-42a7-9cd7-971d529a7321 destroying container for instance a2de1dff-2ea3-4cc2-47db-6e54
2024-09-16T07:43:53.711+0000 [API/11] STDOUT Process has crashed with type: "web"
2024-09-16T07:43:53.733+0000 [API/11] STDOUT App instance exited with guid 29f701b7-10bd-4dfe-9ea0-c1a1b6d3189b payload: {"instance"=>"a2de1dff-2ea3-4cc2-47db-6e54", "index"=>0, "cell_id"=>"80e41736-2c4c-42a7-9cd7-971d529a7321", "reason"=>"CRASHED", "exit_description"=>"Codependent step exited", "crash_count"=>1, "crash_timestamp"=>1726472633684041865, "version"=>"4a8848ae-2f50-4f13-b2c5-e8a3a64f44c5"}
2024-09-16T07:43:53.758+0000 [CELL/0] STDOUT Cell 6b4fecb4-665c-4073-9832-853892f693ef creating container for instance ae994730-42d9-4bdf-55a6-bbd7
2024-09-16T07:43:53.881+0000 [PROXY/0] STDOUT Exit status 137

 

On Postgres Side the log looks like this (don't be confused for the timestamps not matching):

 

2024-09-13 16:25:17.775 UTC [239] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:25:17.775 UTC [238] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:46:02.959 UTC [375] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:47:08.495 UTC [391] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:47:08.495 UTC [392] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out

 

There is no explicit firewall configured and the connection to the database itself works - at least for a certain amount of time.

For testing purposes, I subscribed to the BTP Postgres service and created a binding to the application. In this setup, I don't have any connection issues.

I have already tried these things:

  • Using the same Postgres DB version in my own setup as the DB which is provisioned in BTP
  • Use the same (or almost the same) configuration of the database in my own setup as the BTP Postgres DB
  • Tried two different locations for providing my own postgres db

So the question is: Why do I have these connection timeouts? I'm a bit out of ideas what to try next so any help is really appreciated!

Thanks
Andreas

entry.title: User Exit For IW47
entry.content:
hello,

i need to add a field in the input parameters to the IW47 transaction
should i use a user exit? which one should i use?

thanks

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Nala supervising my workspace...

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content: This method allows you to set the display order of UDFs for all users accessing that specific form within SAP Business. Speak to your SAP B1 administrator if you encounter permissions issues or if the settings do not seem to apply to all users as expected

entry.title: Real Talk with Katya and Jerome | Artificial Intelligence Track Leads
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

We’re Katya Reznik and Jerome Simonetti, and we’re excited to introduce ourselves and give you a glimpse of what to expect from the AI track sessions at this year’s TechEd. 

Katya: Originally from Russia, I’ve lived in Israel for the past 9 years. I love wandering in the desert, snowboarding, diving in cenotes and seas, and exploring nature. I travel a lot, learn languages, make pottery, enjoy the rain, love all dogs and people with a sense of humor. I’m part of the Business AI unit and often talk about technologies and our future:) 

Jerome: I’m Product Expert at SAP Business AI Product Management team. I like to go on multi-day hikes with my wife. My vacation is a subtle balance between visiting cities and museums and going hiking. In terms of art, I'm quite eclectic. I love music (jazz, lyrical, opera) and I regularly go to concerts. I love painting, especially the Italian grandmasters of the 15th and 16th century.  I also like comics. I am married. I have 3 children who are adults. 2 of them have children! 

With our AI track you will learn how innovations like Joule, our generative AI copilot, and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming key areas such as finance, HR, supply chain, and IT. Discover real-world success stories, expert insights, and actionable ways to enhance decision-making and agility in your organization. 

We look forward to sharing insights and innovations in AI with you at TechEd! 

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

The transformative potential of AI and how it can revolutionize the way businesses operate is truly fascinating. It’s an exciting opportunity to dive deep into topics of generative AI, machine learning, knowledge graphs and much more. Specializing in this track allows us to explore/witness real-world success stories and expert insights. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

This track perfectly captures the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit by offering both hands-on experience and real-world applications. The two jump-start sessions bring AI from theory to practical implementation: Set up Joule to answer your own policy documents and Build your own generative AI scenario for your use case. Additionally, our sessions with customers like Sappi and the City of Hamburg and a partner MSG showcase how AI is already driving innovation and delivering results in real business environments. 

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar): 

  • AI100 - Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results 
  • AI105 - Examining how to onboard and extend Joule 
  • AI106 - Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub 
  • AI104 - Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot 
  • AI108 - How AI agents can support your business in the future 
  • AI102 - Enhance business predictions with SAP Foundation Model and knowledge graphs 
  • AI101 - Enable more secure and responsible generative AI 

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Curiosity & Questions: Most importantly, come with an open mind and questions. TechEd is about learning, so actively participate in sessions, ask the experts, and explore new ideas. 
  • Time Management: With so many sessions, it's easy to get overwhelmed. Build your agenda and don’t forget to schedule breaks to keep yourself fresh and engaged throughout the day. 
  • Comfortable Workspace ️: Since TechEd is virtual, set up a comfortable and quiet workspace that helps you focus. A good chair and reliable internet are important to stay connected throughout the sessions. 

We can’t wait to have you join us at TechEd Virtual! Together, we’ll explore how AI can transform business operations and create real impact. Register now, and let’s shape the future of AI together! We’re excited to engage with you, answer your questions, and learn from each other along the way. See you there!

entry.title: Re: Connecting external Output Management System to SAP Print Queue in SAP Cloud Solutions
entry.content:
@JP_Mendoza,
How is your architecture / implementation for "S4 Public Cloud using SAP BTP print service"?
Can you share more details on that? 
If SAP print API is used and “Notification type” is set to “Single notification”, a notification will be sent each time an item is created. See https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/b5670aaaa2364a29935f40b16499972d/754b6cdbb0d545918a7387c1ccaa7811.html?version=202310.001 
Is there any specific reason why you don't connect S4 Public Cloud system directly to Cloud Print Manager? Note: Cloud Print Manager does not support PUSH notifications (SAP_COM_0647).
Some external products support PUSH notifications (Certified Function: Notification for print queue job-items). See https://www.sap.com/dmc/exp/2013_09_adpd/enEN/#/solutions?filters=v:8048d886-1118-480e-876a-2548ffa10139.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content: Facing issue with below task, I get and error saying Customer is not linked to the Card. I did add the payment method to the customer, this was a trial card as mentioned.

entry.title: Re: Get individual primary key values from CDHDR table
entry.content:
Hello Noor,

 

Are you using SLT? This question doesn't seem related to SLT. 
If you have more information on the SAP product you're using I can try to direct you to the correct location.

 

Best Regards,
Michael
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Not able to see the batch and batch stock of Split Valuated materials in FSM.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently facing a similar issue. Any help is very welcome.

entry.title: Re: CDS VIew Multiple Row for Status
entry.content:
I solved , i think . 
Problem was i forgot to do the Partitioning to start new count for each outbound, schedule line. 

ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY outbound, sched_line ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(ZSACCONTROLCON.latestchanged, 'YYYYMMDDHH24MISS') DESC) AS DateRank,

entry.title: Re: WCM Control Key
entry.content: Hi Matjaz, My question is about configuring WCM control key . What parameters need to be set for WCM control key ? Supposing if I assign another control keyPMIN/PMEX , system will not trigger WCM feature, why ?

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content: Earlier I edited my form as LoHa showed. The form is OK for me, but not other users. My user is superuser.

entry.title: Apprirition d'un sigle croix rouge sur l'espace de travail
entry.content:
Hello,

One user is seeing a small white cross on a red circle next to the name of their workspace.
At first it does not bother them at all to work on their models, but we would like to understand the meaning of this alert.

Thank you


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Apprirition d'un sigle croix rouge sur l'espace de tr...
entry.content:
Hello,

It usually happens when the security level is not set to "No restrictions" anymore.
If any of your named paths point to folders with more permissive write permissions, then your PowerDesigner installation is deemed to be insecure, your local workspace root displays a red shield.

Change the location of each of the insecure named path folders (or constrain their permissions) appropriately. If all of your named paths point to secure folders, your workspace root displays a green shield.

Kind regards,
SAP Support
